level_2.origins_history:
Sauvignon Blanc originated in the Loire Valley of France, where it has been cultivated for centuries. DNA analysis confirmed that Sauvignon Blanc crossed naturally with Cabernet Franc to produce Cabernet Sauvignon, likely in the Gironde region during the 17th or 18th century.

The variety's name derives from 'sauvage' (wild), possibly referencing its vigorous growth habit or the wild, herbaceous character of its wines. Historical records from the Loire date to the 18th century, though the variety certainly existed earlier.

Sauvignon Blanc remained primarily a French variety until the late 20th century, when New Zealand transformed its global image. Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc, first planted in the 1970s, created an entirely new style that captured world markets and made the variety an international sensation. Today it ranks among the world's most popular white grapes.

genetic_lineage.overview: Sauvignon Blanc is an ancient French variety that crossed with Cabernet Franc to produce Cabernet Sauvignon. It is also a parent of several other varieties.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parent varieties have been identified. Sauvignon Blanc appears to be an ancient indigenous variety of southwestern France.

offspring.name: Cabernet Sauvignon
offspring.notes: DNA confirmed Sauvignon Blanc crossed with Cabernet Franc to produce Cabernet Sauvignon.

mutations.description: Pink-skinned mutation producing fuller, less aromatic wines. Found in Loire and Bordeaux.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Sauvignon Blanc's role as a parent of Cabernet Sauvignon demonstrates how natural crossings created some of the world's most important varieties.

clones.name: Clone 297
clones.characteristics: Widely planted French clone with good aromatic intensity. Balanced yields and typical varietal character.

clones.name: Clone 316
clones.characteristics: Selected for aromatic intensity with citrus and grapefruit character. Lower yields than 297.

clones.name: Clone 376
clones.characteristics: More recent selection focusing on tropical fruit expression. Good for warmer sites.

clones.name: Marlborough Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections adapted to Marlborough conditions. Intense tropical and herbaceous character. The distinctive Marlborough style.

terroir.effect: The classic soil of Sancerre and Chablis. Fossil-rich limestone produces wines of mineral intensity and elegance. Cool temperatures preserve acidity.

wine_character.aromatics: Citrus, flint, chalk, white flowers, gooseberry

terroir.effect: Found in Pouilly-Fumé and parts of Sancerre. Produces the most mineral, long-lived wines with distinctive smoky, flinty character.
Common in Bordeaux and parts of New Zealand. Excellent drainage produces aromatic, ripe wines.
Marlborough's river valley soils. Free-draining with good moisture retention. Produces intensely aromatic wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Highly susceptible to powdery mildew, requiring vigilant spray programs.
Thin skins make it susceptible. Used beneficially for sweet wines in Sauternes.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Grey rot problematic; noble rot used for dessert wines

resistances.notes: Thrives in cool climates where it can maintain acidity and develop varietal character.

disease_profile.general_notes: Sauvignon Blanc is an early-budding, early-ripening variety that performs best in cool to moderate climates. It is vigorous and requires careful canopy management to prevent excessive shading and maintain varietal character. The variety's signature aromatics come from pyrazines (bell pepper, herbaceous) and thiols (tropical fruit, passion fruit), which develop differently depending on climate and winemaking choices.

regional_expressions.style_summary: The historic benchmark for Loire Sauvignon Blanc. These neighboring appellations produce mineral, elegant wines with citrus and herbal notes. Pouilly-Fumé's flinty soils give distinctive smoky character. Both age better than most Sauvignon Blancs.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, gooseberry, flint, herbs, white flowers

regional_expressions.style_summary: The New World benchmark that transformed Sauvignon Blanc's global profile. Intense aromatics of passion fruit, grapefruit, and fresh-cut grass. High acidity and pure fruit expression. The style that launched a thousand imitators.

typical_character.aromatics: Passion fruit, grapefruit, grass, gooseberry, tropical

regional_expressions.style_summary: Bordeaux's premier white wine appellation, where Sauvignon Blanc is typically blended with Sémillon and often oak-aged. Produces fuller, more complex wines than Loire examples, capable of long aging.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, herbs, oak spice, honey with age

regional_expressions.style_summary: South African Sauvignon Blanc bridges Loire elegance and New Zealand intensity. Cool coastal regions like Elgin and Constantia produce wines with both tropical fruit and mineral character.
